name: ExtensionEncounterHistory_Location_Form
title: R5: The physical type of the location (usually the level in the location hierarchy - bed, room, ward, virtual etc.) (new)
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `EncounterHistory.location.form` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`EncounterHistory.location.form` 0..1 `CodeableConcept`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
*  R5: `EncounterHistory.location.form` 0..1 `CodeableConcept`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `EncounterHistory.location.form` has a context of Encounter.location based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `Encounter`.
Element `EncounterHistory.location.form` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: R5: The physical type of the location (usually the level in the location hierarchy - bed, room, ward, virtual etc.) (new)
element.comment:
Element `EncounterHistory.location.form` has a context of Encounter.location based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `Encounter`.
Element `EncounterHistory.location.form` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
This information is de-normalized from the Location resource to support the easier understanding of the encounter resource and processing in messaging or query.

There may be many levels in the hierachy, and this may only pic specific levels that are required for a specific usage scenario.

element.short: The physical type of the location (usually the level in the location hierarchy - bed, room, ward, virtual etc.)
element.definition: This will be used to specify the required levels (bed/ward/room/etc.) desired to be recorded to simplify either messaging or query.
element.comment:
This information is de-normalized from the Location resource to support the easier understanding of the encounter resource and processing in messaging or query.
